title: Chinese satellite manufacturer Hongqing raises $191 million
summary: Hongqing Technology, a satellite manufacturing affiliate of Landspace, raised $191 million, marking one of the largest funding rounds for a Chinese commercial satellite manufacturer.

title: Perovskite solar panel startup Verde Technologies shifts focus to space
summary: Perovskite solar panel startup Verde Technologies is shifting its focus from rooftop applications to space to commercialize its thin-film solar panels for orbital data centers and large satellite constellations.

title: Isar Aerospace to launch German-built Planet imaging satellite
summary: Isar Aerospace secured a contract with Planet's German subsidiary to launch an imaging satellite, showcasing Germany's end-to-end space capabilities. This marks a significant milestone for both the German space industry and commercial satellite deployment.

title: Polish space tech company Sybilla Technologies secures funds to enter U.S. market
summary: Sybilla Technologies, a Polish space tech company, secured a $10 million investment from BGK and 3TS Capital Partners to expand into the U.S. market. The funding aims to support the company's growth and international presence.

title: Making satellite communications more resilient
summary: Kymeta's Ryan Stevenson discusses the benefits and challenges of multi-orbit and multi-band connectivity in a single satellite communications terminal with SpaceNews' Mike Gruss.

title: Final Atlas 5 Amazon Leo mission launches
summary: An Atlas 5 rocket launched on July 2, carrying a set of Amazon's Leo satellites. This was the final mission of the Atlas 5 vehicle to carry a satellite payload.

title: Report links Starliner problems to overconfidence and unrealistic schedules
summary: A new report attributes Boeing's Starliner technical issues to overconfidence, unrealistic schedules, and NASA's insufficient oversight. The CST-100 Starliner has faced persistent problems during its development.

title: FCC to vote on satellite licensing overhaul July 22
summary: The FCC will vote on July 22 to overhaul its satellite licensing process, aiming to streamline applications amid growing constellation plans. The changes seek to create a more efficient 'licensing assembly line' for complex satellite projects.

title: Unseen threats overhead: Drones endanger U.S. rocket launch sites
summary: Drones pose a significant threat to U.S. rocket launch sites, potentially causing millions in delays and affecting multiple launch providers. The issue highlights safety concerns for both commercial and government space operations.

title: Vantor offers up-to-date imagery with WorldView 3D
summary: Vantor, formerly Maxar Intelligence, introduced WorldView 3D on July 1 to deliver high-definition and up-to-date imagery to customers. The new offering emphasizes the importance of data currency in the satellite imaging market.

title: America at 500: Where will we be in space in 2276?
summary: The article speculates about the future of space exploration in 2276, 250 years from now, comparing it to the U.S.'s early days. It questions where humanity might stand in space technology by that time.

title: SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket launches 24 Starlink satellites from California (video)
summary: A SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket successfully launched 24 Starlink satellites from Vandenberg Space Force Base in California. This marks another milestone in SpaceX's ongoing deployment of its Starlink satellite constellation.

title: SpaceX fires up all 6 of Starship's engines ahead of 13th test flight (video)
summary: SpaceX successfully tested all six engines of its Starship vehicle for a full minute at its Starbase facility, marking a key milestone ahead of the 13th test flight.

title: 'Silo' season 3: Release date & how to watch Apple TV's sensational post-apocalyptic saga
summary: Apple TV+ has announced the release date for the third season of the post-apocalyptic series 'Silo'. The show will return to explore deeper mysteries of the Silo world.

title: Apollo 11 landing site, a cosmic Eagle and a blue-white star: 4 night sky targets to celebrate America 250
summary: The article highlights four celestial targets in the night sky, including the Apollo 11 landing site, to celebrate America's 250th anniversary. It encourages stargazers to observe these uniquely American astronomical sights.

title: 'Stellar death is not the end': James Webb Space Telescope glimpses the fate of the solar system in a weird exoplanet orbiting a dead star
summary: The James Webb Space Telescope observed an exoplanet orbiting a dead star, offering insights into the potential fate of our solar system. This discovery is compared to a 'time machine' revealing the distant future of Earth and other planets.

title: Astronomers discover a potentially habitable planet just 25 light-years away. 'This one is exciting'
summary: Astronomers discovered a potentially habitable planet 25 light-years away orbiting within its star's habitable zone. The planet could potentially support liquid water and possibly life.

title: How to invade planet Earth: A sci-fi movie alien's guide to bringing the human race to its knees
summary: A humorous article discusses sci-fi movie tropes about alien invasions as a guide for hypothetical extraterrestrial conquerors of Earth. It references popular films like 'Independence Day' and 'Invasion of the Body Snatchers' as sources of inspiration.

title: 'Flying saucer' arrives at NASA for Artemis 3 moon mission in time for World UFO Day | Space photo of the day for July 2, 2026
summary: A 'flying saucer' spacecraft has arrived at NASA for the upcoming Artemis 3 moon mission, coinciding with World UFO Day. The vehicle is now in position for integration ahead of its lunar mission.

title: NASA’s Hubble Spots Star-Spangled Cosmic Scene
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the globular cluster Messier 3 (M3) featuring over 500,000 stars in red, white, and blue hues. This cosmic scene highlights the dense and colorful stellar population of the cluster.

title: NASA’s Artemis II Breaks Agency Streaming Record
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission achieved record-breaking viewership with over 149.4 million views across its live coverage, including the launch, lunar flyby, and splashdown. The 24/7 streams on NASA-owned platforms demonstrated strong global engagement throughout the mission.

title: Good Morning, Earth!
summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams captured a photo of an orbital sunrise from the International Space Station on June 26, 2026. The ISS completes 16 orbits around Earth daily, experiencing 16 sunrises and sunsets.

title: NASA’s Webb Reveals Stars Sparking to Life in Cosmic Celebration
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope has captured infrared images of newly forming stars in the FS Tau system, revealing details previously obscured by dust. The image also showcases numerous background galaxies, adding to the cosmic spectacle.

title: What’s Up: July 2026 Skywatching Tips from NASA
summary: NASA shares skywatching tips for July 2026, highlighting a Moon-and-planets alignment, a returning comet, Milky Way visibility, and Saturn's rings at a new angle.

title: NASA’s Chandra Examines Milky Way at Arms’ Length
summary: NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory reveals that the outer spiral arms of the Milky Way may extend farther than previously believed, potentially altering astronomers' understanding of the galaxy's structure.

title: NASA Seeks Volunteers for New Yearlong Simulated Moon, Mars Mission
summary: NASA is recruiting volunteers for a yearlong simulated mission to the Moon or Mars, starting in August 2027. The study will take place at Johnson Space Center in Houston to gather insights for future crewed deep space missions.

title: LINK Spacecraft Set for Mission to Boost NASA’s Swift Observatory
summary: A robotic servicing spacecraft named LINK, developed by Katalyst Space, is set to launch on July 2 to boost the orbit of NASA's Neil Gehrels Swift Observatory. This mission marks the first attempt to raise the observatory's orbit.

title: NASA’s Webb Studies How Planet Survived Death of its Star
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope is studying how a planet survived the death of its star, offering insights into the future of our solar system. The observations reveal new details about a Sun-like star's expansion into a red giant and its planetary system.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4934-4940: In the Land of the Polygons
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover team plans activities for sols 4934-4940, focusing on a light-toned, smooth unit observed from orbital imagery. The rover's science team conducts operations in this new terrain.

title: NASA announces new lander contracts for Moon Base Phase One
summary: NASA announced four new lunar lander contracts as part of its Moon Base Phase One initiative. The contracts aim to support future lunar missions and establish a sustainable human presence on the Moon.

title: Blue Origin accelerates LC-36 recovery via hybrid plan
summary: Blue Origin is accelerating the recovery of Launch Complex-36 at Cape Canaveral using a hybrid plan following a recent anomaly that severely damaged the facility during the New Glenn launch. The company is working to restore operations at the site amid ongoing investigations.

title: Launch Preview: Swift rescue mission, final Atlas satellite launch
summary: A rescue mission for the Swift observatory is planned alongside the final launch of an Atlas satellite. Multiple orbital launches are scheduled globally this week.

title: Pegasus XL set to air launch Swift Boost Mission to save NASA space telescope
summary: A Northrop Grumman Pegasus XL rocket is set to air launch the Swift Boost Mission to save a NASA space telescope. This marks the rocket's first mission since 2021.

title: Euclid studies Milky Way center, supports future Roman observations
summary: ESA's Euclid space telescope captured a new image of the Milky Way's center, aiding future observations by NASA's Roman Space Telescope. The data will help study galactic structures and support joint scientific missions.

title: Ship 40 completes single-engine static fire; Booster 20 and Pad 2 see new work
summary: SpaceX conducted a single-engine static fire test for Ship 40. Booster 20 and Pad 2 are undergoing new modifications.

title: Space Shuttle Endeavour displayed vertically in new exhibit at California Science Center
summary: Space Shuttle Endeavour has been displayed vertically in a new exhibit at the California Science Center. The exhibit aims to educate the public about the shuttle's history and contributions.

title: Launch Preview: Pegasus XL to launch amongst Falcon 9 and Chinese missions
summary: This week's launch manifest includes six orbital launches worldwide, featuring the first Pegasus XL launch alongside Falcon 9 and Chinese missions.

title: SpaceX tests upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle with demonstration mission on Tuesday
summary: SpaceX conducted a demonstration mission for its upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle using a Falcon 9 rocket. The test flight took place early Tuesday morning.

title: Canadarm2 repairs planned as CRS-34 departs ISS
summary: The Expedition 74 crew on the ISS is planning repairs for the Canadarm2 as the CRS-34 mission departs. This follows a busy month of activities aboard the station.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The news source is a B2B website focused on the technology, engineering, and construction sectors. No specific space industry news is provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements. The publication marks five decades of significant milestones in space exploration and technology.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to discuss the implementation of CM25, the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025, focusing on industrial opportunities and thematic priorities. These events aim to inform and engage the industrial community on follow-up activities.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm designed for space applications, highlighting its versatility and potential applications. It emphasizes the significance of this technology in advancing robotic capabilities in space.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway, supporting scientific research in space. The programme facilitates participation of research institutes in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are helping ESA test materials for spacecraft to ensure they survive space conditions. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section relies on innovative ideas from these contributors.

title: Inside the Deepening Ties Between Europe and the Middle East
summary: Europe's strained geopolitical ties with the US have led to strengthened space industry collaborations with Middle Eastern companies. This shift is creating new opportunities for partnerships in the global space sector.

title: True Anomaly Approaches, Images Rocket Lab Craft
summary: True Anomaly completed an imaging task for SSC in 61 hours after receiving the order, which was due within 72 hours. The mission involved capturing images of a Rocket Lab spacecraft.

title: Exclusive: Austria Taps R-Space for Its Second Military Sat
summary: Austria has selected R-Space to develop its second military satellite, named Aurora, as part of a series of government-backed demonstrations to test new orbital technologies.

title: Exclusive: Lunar Forge Unveils Plans to Build Lunar Infrastructure Out of Dust
summary: Lunar Forge has revealed plans to construct lunar infrastructure using regolith to reduce costs for fission reactors. The company aims to use lunar dust to build support structures and shielding for reactor cores.

title: NASA Praises Blue Origin’s Mishap Progress, Announces Moon Base Contracts
summary: NASA praised Blue Origin's progress in addressing past mishaps and announced contracts for moon base missions. The agency aims for monthly landings with NASA payloads using updated lander designs.

title: Payload Field Guide: CLPS Missions
summary: The article discusses CLPS (Commercial Lunar Payload Services) missions, focusing on landers and their role in upcoming Moon missions. It provides an overview of payloads and preparations for lunar exploration.

title: Exclusive: Satellogic Expands AI Apps with SpaceKnow Partnership
summary: Satellogic has partnered with SpaceKnow to develop AI applications using Satellogic's satellite imagery. The collaboration aims to enhance geospatial intelligence analysis capabilities.

title: Rocket Lab Enters the Satcom Market With $8B Iridium Deal
summary: Rocket Lab has entered the satcom market through an $8 billion deal with Iridium. This marks a significant expansion of Rocket Lab's business beyond launch services.

title: Firefly Targets Swedish Launch in 2028 Under New Franchise Model
summary: Firefly Aerospace plans to launch its Alpha rocket from SSC Space’s Esrange Space Center in Sweden by 2028 under a new franchise model. This marks Firefly's expansion into the European market.

title: US Army Establishes a Space Operations Branch
summary: The US Army has established a dedicated Space Operations Branch to enhance the integration of space-based technologies with military operations. This move aims to strengthen modern warfare capabilities by leveraging advanced space assets.

title: Amazon Leo Constellation Ready to Support Initial Service After ULA Mission
summary: Amazon's Leo constellation has achieved enough satellites in orbit to support initial service across key latitudes following a successful ULA launch. This marks a significant milestone for Amazon's satellite internet ambitions.

title: Isar Aerospace Lands Big Win With Planet Launch Contract
summary: Isar Aerospace secured a launch contract with Planet to launch one of Planet's next-generation Pelican satellites, potentially as early as late 2026. This marks a significant milestone for the German launcher currently in development.

title: Mitsubishi Electric to Acquire Infostellar
summary: Mitsubishi Electric has announced its intent to acquire Infostellar, a prominent company in the Japanese space sector. The acquisition aims to strengthen Mitsubishi Electric's position in the space industry.

title: Vodafone Ireland Conducts Emergency Services D2D Call With AST SpaceMobile JV
summary: Vodafone Ireland successfully conducted a test call with AST SpaceMobile's satellite constellation to evaluate connectivity for emergency services and first responders. The test is part of ongoing efforts to enhance communication capabilities in critical scenarios.

title: The Internet in Space: The Apps that Dominate Satellite Traffic and Why it Matters
summary: The article discusses the applications dominating satellite internet traffic and their significance for non-government use as thousands of new satellites are launched. It explores what users actually do on satellite networks and why it matters beyond governmental applications.

title: Dish Satellite TV and Wireless Businesses File for Chapter 11 Bankruptcy
summary: Dish Network's satellite TV and wireless businesses filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy to facilitate the transition of its Dish Wireless operations after spectrum acquisitions. The move aims to restructure debt and streamline operations amid financial challenges.

title: Ovzon Wins New Defense Deal with European NATO Nation
summary: Ovzon secured a $7.1 million defense contract with a European NATO nation for its satellite communications solution. The deal is valued at 74 million Swedish krona.

title: Unseenlabs Orders 2 Cubesats from GomSpace
summary: Unseenlabs ordered two additional cubesats from GomSpace for a contract worth 2.2 million euros ($2.5 million). GomSpace announced the deal on Wednesday.

title: Lite Coms Hires Calspan Exec Greg Campbell as President
summary: Lite Coms has appointed Greg Campbell, a former Calspan executive, as its new president. Campbell will lead the company's daily operations and focus on expanding manufacturing capabilities and investments.

title: Astrobotic, Firefly Aerospace, and Intuitive Machines Win NASA Lunar Delivery Missions
summary: Astrobotic, Firefly Aerospace, and Intuitive Machines have secured NASA contracts to deliver payloads to the Moon as part of efforts to establish a lunar base. The missions support NASA's broader goals for sustainable lunar exploration.
